# Collab Platform

> A shared workspace for a small team. It puts chat, direct messages, task boards, notes, reminders, a whiteboard, embedded documents, and voice and video calls in one place, so a small group can run a project without juggling five separate tools.

Collab Platform is a web app. It is account-based: you sign in, then work inside servers (teams) made of channels, alongside direct messages with other people.

## What you can do

- Chat in text channels, with live typing indicators, presence, image uploads, and message editing and deletion.
- Send direct messages to anyone by their @username, even if you do not share a team yet.
- Run a task board (to do, in progress, done) with assignees and due dates. Tasks assigned to you show up on your desk under "Waiting on you".
- Keep shared notes that save as you type and sync live to everyone in the space.
- Track reminders with due times.
- Draw together on a live multiplayer whiteboard.
- Embed a Google Doc, Sheet, or Slides file into a channel.
- Start a peer-to-peer voice or video call in a voice channel.
- Keep a private cubicle: a personal space only you can see.

## How it is organized

- **Desk**: your home view. Shows what is waiting on you, your teams, and your direct messages.
- **Servers (teams)**: contain channels. Owners and admins add or remove channels and invite people with a link.
- **Channels**: each has a type (text, voice/video, whiteboard, tasks, notes, reminders, docs, or cubicle).
- **Direct messages**: one-to-one conversations, reachable from the message icon on the left rail.
- **Profile**: set a unique @username, a display name, an avatar, and a short status line.

## Technical notes

- Built with Next.js and React on the front end.
- Supabase handles auth, the Postgres database, row-level security, storage, and realtime.
- Voice and video use browser WebRTC in a peer-to-peer mesh, signalled over Supabase Realtime. No separate media server.

## Access

Using Collab Platform requires an account. Sign in with Google or with an email and password at /login.
